refrain.setup: A little line can find the way.
refrain.climaxMutation: A brave blue line can lead us home.

beats.storyText: Theo loved his battered crayon box most of all, especially the blue crayon worn flat from drawing rivers.
beats.pageTurnHook: Then his blue line wriggled off the paper.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo kneels at a low art table in a cozy room, paint-smudged smock on, battered crayon box open, drawing a blue river on paper.

beats.storyText: The blue line slid across the floor and curled around a lost toy boat waiting under the chair.
beats.pageTurnHook: Theo wondered whether a drawing could know where to go.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo watches a blue crayon line curl gently from paper across the floor toward a small toy boat under a chair.

beats.storyText: He whispered, A little line can find the way, but his hand shook when the line reached the hallway shadows.
beats.pageTurnHook: The toy boat bumped forward anyway.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo stands at the edge of a softly lit hallway, holding his blue crayon and toy boat, uncertain but curious.

beats.storyText: Theo drew bridges, stepping stones, and a loop-de-loop sky, and the blue line carried the boat through every pretend place.
beats.pageTurnHook: Until the crayon snapped in two.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo draws an imaginative indoor river path with bridges and stepping stones, guiding the toy boat through playful painted scenery.

beats.storyText: With half a crayon and a big blank wall ahead, Theo felt too small to finish the river.
beats.pageTurnHook: The little line waited for him to choose.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo sits before a large blank paper taped to the wall, holding two pieces of blue crayon, worried but thinking hard.

beats.storyText: Theo pressed both blue pieces together and cried, A brave blue line can lead us home, drawing a shining river back to the art table.
beats.pageTurnHook: The toy boat sailed straight into a hug.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo draws with both broken blue crayon pieces at once, a glowing blue river sweeping warmly through the room toward the art table and toy boat.

beats.storyText: Now Theo keeps the snapped blue crayon in front, because some lines grow braver after they break.
beats.pageTurnHook: And every new river begins with a tiny wiggle.
beats.illustrationBrief: Theo smiles at his art table, the repaired blue crayon pieces beside the toy boat, a finished river drawing glowing softly in afternoon light.
